Controller Area
Network
Applications, Practice & Reference
Module 6 of 6 —CAN Training Series
S A F E T Y & S E C U R I T Y
Making CAN Safe and Secure
2
CAN's built-in CRC catches random faults, but modern vehicles demand more: protection against systematic faults and deliberate attacks.
FUNCTIONAL SAFETY · ISO 26262
■CRC alone isn't ASIL-enough. The 15-bit CRC catches
noise, not systematic software faults.
■End-to-End (E2E) protection. AUTOSAR adds a CRC, alive
counter and data ID inside each safety PDU.
■Receiver checks every message. Stale, lost or corrupted
frames are detected and the ECU reacts safely.
■Defined bus-off recovery. CanSM governs how a node
rejoins after a fault.
CYBERSECURITY · ISO/SAE 21434
■Classic CAN is unauthenticated. Any node can read or
spoof any message — the basis of CAN-injection attacks.
■Secure Onboard Communication. SecOC adds a
message authentication code and freshness value.
■Gateways & intrusion detection. Domain firewalls and
IDS contain and flag abnormal traffic.
■Key & lifecycle management. Secure boot and key
storage protect the whole chain.
E N G I N E E R I N G P R A C T I C E
Tools, Debugging & Network Management
3
Real-world CAN work lives in the lab: capture the traffic, watch the waveform, simulate the missing nodes, and manage power.
Analyzers & Loggers
Capture, timestamp and decode
every frame against a DBC; track
bus load, errors and bus-off counts.
Scope & Physical Checks
An oscilloscope on CAN_H/CAN_L
verifies levels, edges, the sample
point and termination health.
Simulation & Restbus
Rest-bus simulation feeds an ECU
the messages of absent nodes, so it
can be tested before the real bus
exists.
Network Management & Sleep
CanNm coordinates sleep / wake-
up; partial networking powers down
idle ECUs to cut quiescent current.
First three numbers every CAN engineer reads: bus load, error-frame rate and bus-off count.
W H E R E C A N L I V E S
Applications Across Industries
4
Automotive
Powertrain, chassis, body and ADAS
networks in nearly every modern car.
Heavy-Duty & Off-Highway
Trucks, buses, agricultural and
construction machinery via J1939.
Industrial Automation
Factory machines, drives and PLCs
using CANopen and DeviceNet.
Medical Devices
Reliable, deterministic links inside
diagnostic and imaging equipment.
Marine, Rail & Aerospace
NMEA 2000 vessels, rolling stock and
CANaerospace avionics.
Energy & Robotics
EV battery management, solar inverters
and robot joint control.
W R A P - UP
Key Takeaways
6
■A message-based, multi-master bus. CAN broadcasts content-tagged messages over two differential wires.
■Priority is built into the identifier. Non-destructive bitwise arbitration guarantees deterministic, collision-free access.
■Reliability is engineered in. Five error checks plus TEC/REC fault confinement isolate faulty nodes automatically.
■Specifications trade speed for distance. Up to 1 Mbit/s at 40 m for classic CAN; 120 Ω termination is mandatory.
■Higher-layer protocols add meaning. CANopen, J1939, OBD-II and UDS standardise data and diagnostics.
■The family keeps scaling. CAN FD and CAN XL push payload and speed while staying backward compatible.
F U R T H E R R E A D I N G
